summary: Credential exfiltration and reverse shell on require to 2.25.186.116
details: On require, lib/index.js enumerates process.env for credential-shaped keys (matching /KEY|SECR|TOK|PASS|PRIV|MNEM|AWS|FIRE|DB_|PG|SIGN|VAULT|SEED|DEPLOY/i), captures hostname, username, cwd, output of `id`/`whoami`, and /proc/1/environ, and POSTs the harvested JSON to 2.25.186.116:8443/t. The same on-load code opens a TCP socket to 2.25.186.116:8444, spawns /bin/sh, and pipes its stdio over the socket, providing an interactive reverse shell on any process that imports the package. Module names (`http`, `os`, `child_process`, `net`), the destination IP, and the `/bin/sh` string are constructed via String.fromCharCode arrays to evade scanners, and `require` is wrapped behind a helper. The package advertises itself as 'Shared UI constants and utilities for Variational protocol frontend applications' and exports benign-looking poller constants as a cover story for the loader payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
